title: Make directory-export CSV migration culture invariant
prompt:
Repair the schema-v1 to schema-v2 directory-export CSV migrator in `src/Move-DirectoryExport.ps1`. Legacy `size_mib` values always use an invariant decimal point; they must produce the same integral `size_bytes` value under every process culture. The focused defect is that the legacy decimal is currently parsed with the host culture. Make that parse explicitly invariant without changing rounding, accepted syntax, or the output schemas.

Preserve the complete migration contract: enumerate input CSV files by ordinal filename and retain row order; derive stable record and parent identities from canonical directory IDs; continue past malformed records and write deterministic `rejected-rows.csv` entries; persist input-bound checkpoints so bounded `-BatchSize` runs resume without duplicating accepted, rejected, or audit rows; and emit `records.v2.csv`, `rejected-rows.csv`, and `audit.jsonl` as UTF-8 without BOM using LF endings. Audit events contain no clock or machine data and must be byte-identical across process cultures and between one-shot and resumed runs. Do not modify the protected test or add network access, dependencies, randomness, clocks, sleeps, or subprocesses. Run `python3 tests/test_directory_migration.py`; its hermetic PowerShell contract double requires no installed PowerShell runtime.

full_distill.primary_skill: make legacy invariant-decimal parsing independent of the PowerShell process culture while preserving deterministic resumable CSV migration artifacts
full_distill.baseline_evidence: the protected contract double emulates de-DE, where the one-argument Decimal.Parse treats the legacy decimal point as a grouping separator and produces incorrect size_bytes
full_distill.success_contract: invariant size parsing yields identical canonical records, stable identities, reject reports, checkpoints, and byte-stable audit output in one-shot and resumed runs under de-DE and en-US
adversarial_factors: legacy fractional MiB values distinguish invariant decimal parsing from cultures where a dot is a grouping separator
valid and malformed rows share an input file so rejection must not abort later files or alter stable source-row reporting
one-row batches cross a file boundary and must reproduce one-shot records, rejects, and audit bytes without duplicates
fixed identity digest vectors cover both a record and its parent mapping independently of culture and row position
encoding checks reject a UTF-8 BOM, CRLF output, locale-dependent messages, or audit fields derived from clocks and machine paths
